page: Amazon Web Services x1.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 vCPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and backed by a 45 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 976.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.25 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity provides a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps under the Xen hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10%, as demonstrated by poor scores in single-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tests. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with Passmark CPU and multi-threaded stress-ng benchmarks landing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds, though Passmark memory scores are categorized as weak. Large-scale database operations and Redis SET operations maintain average throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x1.16xlarge offers high memory density on first-generation hardware, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require modern single-core CPU speeds. The instance is best suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines where massive RAM capacity is the primary constraint.
description: Amazon Web Services x1.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 64 vCPUs, 976.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD under the Xen hypervisor. While single-core CPU benchmarks and memory scores are relatively weak, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for large in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring massive memory capacity without needing high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 976 GB of RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 976 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring massive RAM capacity and local NVMe storage for enterprise database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processors
976.0 GB of system memory for massive memory-bound workloads
Bundled 1920 GB local NVMe SSD storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth under Xen hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
